lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: Do you seek guidance, traveller?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, gimme your best advice.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: You flesh-temples always assume you can receive guidance merely by asking someone for it. But in this land there is a power that can teach us far more than any mere teacher could.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: I speak of the Dark Altar. For generations our wisest scholars have studied it, yet still it has capacities beyond our comprehension.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: The Dark Altar can distort the dimensions, warping the fabric of reality as you perceive it, and even bridging the gap between life and death.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: But you are still a flesh-temple; you require a corporeal body to house your soul, and you perform disgusting bodily functions to maintain it.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: We of Arceuus have learnt, through the power of the Dark Altar, to move beyond the need for such a flesh- temple to house our souls.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: In the crypt below us, we freed our souls from the flesh-temples that had imprisoned them, and we took the form you see now.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: Perhaps, if you allow the Dark Altar to work on your mind, one day you too will be able to ascend to our higher form.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for explaining that.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: I fear you are far from comprehending it. Now, is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I like having a body. It's fun.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: How disgusting - a flesh-temple that worships itself. Now, do you want anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What god do you worship?

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: You sound like the Saradominist peasants from Hosidius, always assuming they must serve one god above all others.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: While the gods are useful for tedious matters such as granting prayers during combat, we of Arceuus have long moved beyond the need to pay them homage.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: This is a place of meditation, where a novice may contemplate the mysteries of the Dark Altar - without venturing too close to its power.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: Saradomin is popular with many uneducated citizens of Kourend, but you may use our church to worship any god you prefer. That would be an ecumenical matter.

lines.speaker: Mofina
lines.text: Now, do you require anything of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm fine.
